root_text.an10.117:1.1: Atha kho saṅgāravo brāhmaṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an10.117:1.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho saṅgāravo brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an10.117:1.3: “kiṁ nu kho, bho gotama, orimaṁ tīraṁ, kiṁ pārimaṁ tīran”ti?
root_text.an10.117:1.4: “Micchādiṭṭhi kho, brāhmaṇa, orimaṁ tīraṁ, sammādiṭṭhi pārimaṁ tīraṁ;
root_text.an10.117:1.5: micchāsaṅkappo orimaṁ tīraṁ, sammāsaṅkappo pārimaṁ tīraṁ;
root_text.an10.117:1.6: micchāvācā orimaṁ tīraṁ, sammāvācā pārimaṁ tīraṁ;
root_text.an10.117:1.7: micchākammanto orimaṁ tīraṁ, sammākammanto pārimaṁ tīraṁ;
root_text.an10.117:1.8: micchāājīvo orimaṁ tīraṁ, sammāājīvo pārimaṁ tīraṁ;
root_text.an10.117:1.9: micchāvāyāmo orimaṁ tīraṁ, sammāvāyāmo pārimaṁ tīraṁ;
root_text.an10.117:1.10: micchāsati orimaṁ tīraṁ, sammāsati pārimaṁ tīraṁ;
root_text.an10.117:1.11: micchāsamādhi orimaṁ tīraṁ, sammāsamādhi pārimaṁ tīraṁ;
root_text.an10.117:1.12: micchāñāṇaṁ orimaṁ tīraṁ, sammāñāṇaṁ pārimaṁ tīraṁ;
root_text.an10.117:1.13: micchāvimutti orimaṁ tīraṁ, sammāvimutti pārimaṁ tīranti.
root_text.an10.117:1.14: Idaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, orimaṁ tīraṁ, idaṁ pārimaṁ tīranti.

translation_text.an10.117:1.1: Then Saṅgārava the brahmin went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an10.117:1.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.an10.117:1.3: “Worthy Gotama, what is the near shore? And what is the far shore?”
translation_text.an10.117:1.4: “Wrong view is the near shore, brahmin, and right view is the far shore.
translation_text.an10.117:1.5: Wrong thought is the near shore, and right thought is the far shore.
translation_text.an10.117:1.6: Wrong speech is the near shore, and right speech is the far shore.
translation_text.an10.117:1.7: Wrong action is the near shore, and right action is the far shore.
translation_text.an10.117:1.8: Wrong livelihood is the near shore, and right livelihood is the far shore.
translation_text.an10.117:1.9: Wrong effort is the near shore, and right effort is the far shore.
translation_text.an10.117:1.10: Wrong mindfulness is the near shore, and right mindfulness is the far shore.
translation_text.an10.117:1.11: Wrong immersion is the near shore, and right immersion is the far shore.
translation_text.an10.117:1.12: Wrong knowledge is the near shore, and right knowledge is the far shore.
translation_text.an10.117:1.13: Wrong freedom is the near shore, and right freedom is the far shore.
translation_text.an10.117:1.14: This is the near shore, and this is the far shore.
translation_text.an10.117:2.2: who cross to the far shore.
translation_text.an10.117:3.1: When the teaching is well explained,
translation_text.an10.117:3.3: are the ones who will cross over
translation_text.an10.117:3.4: Death’s dominion so hard to pass.
translation_text.an10.117:4.2: an astute person should develop the bright.
translation_text.an10.117:4.4: for the seclusion so hard to enjoy,
translation_text.an10.117:5.4: would cleanse themselves of mental corruptions.
translation_text.an10.117:6.1: Those whose minds are rightly developed
translation_text.an10.117:6.6: they are quenched in this world.”
